Full job description
Job Title: Power BI Developer & Analyst

Location: Stoke on Trent (ST4 6NW)

Working Hours: Full time: 9am – 5pm, Monday to Friday

The role:

We’re looking for a Power BI Developer & Analyst to lead our business analytics function and turn data into clear, actionable insights. You’ll work across teams to strengthen commercial understanding, support improvement initiatives, and play a key role in shaping our three-year plan, annual budget, and monthly forecasts.

In this role, you’ll develop high-quality reporting, analyse marketing effectiveness, support patient conversion insights, forecast demand, and help guide decisions on future clinic expansion. You will also contribute as part of the core transaction team during our next reinvestment cycle.

This is a hands-on, high impact role working within a friendly, cross functional environment.

What you’ll need:

You’ll bring proven experience developing and maintaining Power BI reporting for a range of stakeholders, along with strong capability in managing reporting suites, version control, and development processes. Knowledge of data cleansing, data warehousing, or data lake environments is beneficial, and experience working in a multi site organisation will be an advantage. You’ll have advanced Power BI skills, strong Excel and PowerPoint capability, and the ability to influence decision making using clear data and trends. We’re looking for someone who works confidently on their own initiative, generates ideas, and solves business problems creatively. As a clear and adaptable communicator, you’ll collaborate effectively with stakeholders at different analytical levels, thrive in a friendly and flat-structured team, and bring a curious, proactive mindset with the willingness to work across multiple functions.

About Us:

VeinCentre is proud to be the UK’s largest and most established vein care provider, with over 40 clinics nationwide. We have performed over 50,000 procedures since we were founded in 2003. Our purpose is simple: to help every patient Walk Out Happy.

Colleagues join VeinCentre because they want to be part of something that makes a difference to people’s lives. People stay because they share the strong sense of purpose and are passionate about the opportunity to be part of that.

We focus on making every part of working life at VeinCentre the very best experience it can be - our Walk Out Happy purpose applies equally to colleagues.

We are proud to have been recognised by Best Companies as one of The Top 100 Mid-Sized Companies to Work For in the UK, and a Top 10 Health & Social Care Company 2025. We achieved this recognition based on our Employee Engagement Survey, which we carry out every year.

Recognition is a big focus, shining a light on our brilliant colleagues, with monthly shout outs and quarterly VC Stars’ awards. Benefits include having birthdays off, flexible working, length of service awards, free treatments, friends and family discounts and perks sites, including Blue Light Card as we proudly work in partnership with the NHS, in addition treating private patients.

Salary and Benefits:

Benefits include:

33 days annual leave
An additional days’ leave to celebrate your birthday
Company pension scheme
Life Assurance
Enhanced maternity and paternity pay
Access to thousands of discounts through our Perks Site and Blue Light Card
Practical and effective support for your physical, mental and financial health and wellbeing
Discounts on treatments for you, family and friends
Season ticket loan
Cycle to Work Scheme
Referral bonus for recommending friends
Anniversary bonuses at 3, 5, and 10 years
Free parking at most sites
Support for career progression and development
Veincentre is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Job Type: Full-time

Benefits:

Company pension
Cycle to work scheme

Referral programme
Work Location: In person

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I read this as a hands-on analytics role that needs more than dashboard building. It needs someone who can keep reporting reliable, explain what the data means, and help leaders use it for planning, forecasting and growth decisions.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Develop and maintain Power BI reporting

experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I can create practical reporting, not just technical outputs.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Manage reporting suites and development processes
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in environments where reporting and control had to be disciplined, accurate and tied to financial performance, including reconciliation and working-capital improvement.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role needs reporting that is dependable enough to support planning and forecasting.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Influence decision making with data and trends
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve used operational and financial information to support a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation and a £33.3k release of working capital.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is the kind of evidence-led influence this role calls for.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Work across teams and communicate clearly
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led cross-site improvement work and used operational data alongside frontline insight to strengthen execution across different locations.
experienceMappings.relevance: This matches the need to work with stakeholders at different analytical levels.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve supported financial control and reporting in a healthcare setting, where tighter oversight directly affected cash and profit performance.
experienceMappings.relevance: This is relevant because the role feeds the three-year plan, budget and monthly forecasts.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve looked at how to turn fragmented operational information into something more usable and immediate for decision-making.
experienceMappings.relevance: That reflects the kind of practical thinking this role seems to need.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background fits because I’ve worked in healthcare, regulated and multi-site environments where reporting, control and operational judgement all matter. I bring hands-on experience with performance reporting, financial discipline and cross-functional delivery, which lines up well with what this role needs.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend the first 6-12 months tightening reporting quality, improving how insight is presented, and making it easier for teams to use data in planning and forecasting. I’d also expect to help build a clearer view of demand, conversion and performance across sites so decisions on growth and reinvestment are better supported.
companyHighlights: VeinCentre is the UK’s largest and most established vein care provider.
It has been recognised by Best Companies as a Top 100 Mid-Sized Company to Work For and a Top 10 Health & Social Care Company 2025.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people want clear answers, not noise, and where I can stay close to the detail while still keeping the bigger picture in view. The flat, friendly and proactive style described here feels like a good match for how I work.

first90DaysPlan.focus: Understand the data and reporting landscape
first90DaysPlan.detail: I’d get close to the current reporting, the main stakeholders, and the key decisions the business needs to support, so I can see where the gaps and quick wins are.
I’d focus on improving the clarity and reliability of reporting, making sure the outputs are useful for commercial, operational and forecasting conversations.
I’d start contributing more directly to forecast, budget and expansion discussions, using the reporting to highlight trends, risks and opportunities.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring practical analytics, commercial judgement and a hands-on approach to help VeinCentre make better decisions from its data.
closingProofPoints: I’ve built healthcare reporting that brought fragmented operational information into clearer view.
I’ve helped improve profit, cash and working capital through tighter control and reporting.
I’ve worked across multiple sites and used data to support better execution and improvement.
contentNotes: I have kept the evidence focused on reporting, financial control and multi-site improvement because those are the strongest matches in the available CV and evidence bank.
I have not claimed direct Power BI delivery metrics beyond what is supported in the CV evidence.
The advert is stronger on responsibilities than on company detail, so the company section is intentionally concise and limited to supported facts.
